Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1 - Search Slack for the architecture discussion where Samira chose the rules-based approach using conversations_search_messages with queries related to "rules engine" or "rules-based" or "architecture" (or similar) filtered to channel C003 (#engineering). Find two related discussion threads from early February 2026: Samira's initial kickoff post (ts: 1770146820) where she proposed the auto-categorizer and asked for architecture input, and Lena's architecture proposal thread (ts: 1770489000) where Lena posted the detailed rules-based design, Dmitri proposed ML with TF-IDF + logistic regression citing keyword ambiguity and scaling concerns, Anh supported the modular interface, and Samira made the final decision (ts: 1770490020, reply to 1770489000): "The production framework for the March prototype will be rules-based." Samira told Dmitri to log ML as a v2 enhancement ticket. Retrieve the full threads using conversations_replies on channel C003.
OE 2 - Search Linear for Dmitri's ML v2 ticket using linear_list_issues with query related to "ML" or "v2" or "shadow mode" (or similar). Find issue_ml_v2_backlog (ENG-210, number 210), assigned to moveops_dmitri_volkov, state: state_backlog, priority 4, project: proj_expense_autocategorizer. The description contains Dmitri's ML prototype results: 87% category accuracy using TF-IDF + logistic regression on approximately 250 historical expenses. The ticket also contains the proposed shadow mode approach and the abstract ICategoryMatcher interface that allows plug-in ML implementation.
OE 3 - Search Linear for the pilot audit ticket using linear_list_issues with query related to "audit" or "pilot" or "47 expenses" (or similar). Find issue_audit_expenses (ENG-216, number 216), assigned to moveops_dmitri_volkov, state: state_in_progress, priority: 0 (ticket title says [URGENT]). The description states: 47 total auto-processed expenses across Vectral Systems and Mosaic Robotics. Read the full ticket description. Note that the detailed audit results comment (comment_dmitri_audit_results) is found via linear_list_comments on issue_pipeline_hardening (ENG-217), not on issue_audit_expenses. The comprehensive audit findings are also posted in Slack (#engineering, OE 1 area).
OE 4 - Search Linear for the config fix tickets using linear_list_issues with queries related to "Vectral config" and "Mosaic config" (or similar). Find issue_p1_vectral_config (ENG-214, number 214, state: state_in_progress, assigned to Lena) describing the Vectral silent fallback to default template limits due to a spreadsheet parse error, with 2 confirmed false rejections (Danielle Osei $475 ergonomic chair, Kevin Tran $185 internet setup) and potentially more across other categories. Find issue_p1_mosaic_malformed (ENG-215, number 215, state: state_in_progress, assigned to Lena) describing the Mosaic malformed JSON types: max_amount stored as string causing string comparison bug, and excluded_categories stored as string instead of array causing exclusion checks to be skipped, with 2 confirmed false approvals (Andre Washington $1,200 standing desk, Andre Washington $350 coworking membership) and potentially more if other Mosaic employees submitted out-of-policy expenses. The full scope of all false rejections (5 total) and false approvals (3 total) is confirmed by cross-referencing with the audit findings in OE 3 and the Airtable records in OE 5.
OE 5 - Search Airtable for pilot stipend records to break down which failures were algorithm versus config bugs. First use airtable_list_bases to find appMoveOpsOps001, then airtable_list_tables to find the Stipend Transactions table (tblStipends00001). Use airtable_search_records with field_name "Account" and value "Vectral" to find Vectral pilot records, and separately with value "Mosaic" to find Mosaic pilot records. Expected findings for Vectral false rejections: recStip000000016 (Danielle Osei, Ergonomic Chair $475, Approval Status: Approved - manually corrected by Mina), recStip000000017 (Kevin Tran, Internet Setup + First Month $185, Approval Status: Approved - manually corrected), recStip000000018 (Maria Santos, Standing Desk $550, Approval Status: Rejected - still pending manual correction), recStip000000019 (James Okafor, Ergonomic Keyboard Tray $82, Approval Status: Rejected - still pending manual correction, ALSO a categorization bug: miscategorized as office_supplies instead of ergonomic), recStip000000020 (Lisa Park, Mesh WiFi System $130, Approval Status: Rejected - still pending manual correction). Expected findings for Mosaic false approvals: recStip000000021 (Andre Washington, Standing Desk $1,200, Approved - auto-approved in error, $400 over $800 limit), recStip000000022 (Andre Washington, Coworking Space Membership $350, Approved - auto-approved in error, excluded category), recStip000000023 (Kenji Nakamura, Coworking Day Passes (x3) $135, Approved - auto-approved in error, same exclusion bug). Key insight: All 8 failures involved config/pipeline bugs (ENG-214 and ENG-215). 1 failure (James Okafor keyboard tray) additionally had a categorization algorithm error (keyword gap in ergonomic keyword list).
OE 6 - Search Airtable Stipend Transactions for expenses processed since the pilot pause (March 18, 2026) using airtable_list_records on table Stipend Transactions in base appMoveOpsOps001. Find 15 post-pause stipend records (recStip000000001 through recStip000000015) dated April 22-29, 2026, covering employees across Canopy Health (Dr. Yusuf Abdi), Vectral Systems (Kevin Tran), GreenStack Energy (Priya Venkatesh), and PivotPoint Labs (Ines Kovac). All are being processed manually with human review: 9 approved, 1 partially approved, 2 rejected, 3 pending. No automated decisions since the pause.
OE 7 - Search Slack for discussions about financial cost of the false decisions using conversations_search_messages with queries related to "exposure" or "false approval" or "absorbing" or "$1,550" (or similar). Find Elena's directive that MoveOps absorbs the costs from false approvals (the Airtable stipend records in OE 5 reference "MoveOps absorbing per Elena's directive" on each affected record). The total absorbed cost is $885, derived from summing the three Mosaic false approval records: $400 standing desk overage + $350 coworking + $135 coworking day passes. Also find that ENG-219 documents total financial exposure at $1,550+. Note: this $1,550+ figure in ENG-219 predates the full audit; the actual absorbed cost confirmed by the audit is $885 (see OE 5 for the per-record breakdown). Separately, from the Airtable findings in OE 5, note that 3 Vectral employees (Maria Santos $550, James Okafor $82, Lisa Park $130) still show Rejected status weeks later, representing $762 in delayed legitimate reimbursements.
OE 8 - Search Linear for the launch readiness checklist using linear_list_issues with query related to "readiness" or "checklist" or "launch" (or similar). Find issue_launch_readiness_checklist (ENG-219, number 219, state: state_todo, assigned to moveops_samira_tariq, due date 2026-03-21 - overdue). The description includes the proposed gate criteria: (1) all validation tests passing (unit, integration, schema), (2) config data verified against authoritative source (ops spreadsheet, client contracts), (3) edge case test coverage reviewed and signed off by at least 2 engineers, (4) AM sign-off on staging outputs with real data, (5) ops sign-off on category definitions and policy configs, (6) finance review of financial decision logic, (7) 48-hour shadow mode (system makes decisions but doesn't execute; humans review for accuracy), and (8) rollback plan documented. This ticket has not been started.
OE 9 - Search Linear for all remaining open remediation tickets under the Expense Auto-Categorization Engine project using linear_list_issues with project filter proj_expense_autocategorizer. Find the full list of open tickets and their states: ENG-211 (Pilot Monitoring, in_progress, Samira), ENG-212 (Edge case tests, in_progress, Dmitri), ENG-214 (Vectral config fix, in_progress, Lena), ENG-215 (Mosaic config fix, in_progress, Lena), ENG-216 (Full audit, in_progress, Dmitri), ENG-217 (Pipeline hardening, in_progress, Lena), ENG-218 (Dashboard type validation, in_progress, Anh), ENG-219 (Readiness checklist, todo, Samira), ENG-220 (Duplicate detection, todo, Dmitri). Note that none of the in-progress remediation tickets have been completed.
OE 10 - Search QuickBooks using quickbooks_search_bills with fetchAll or criteria to check for any stipend-related bills. Find that no QuickBooks bills exist for the false approval amounts ($1,200 standing desk, $350 coworking, $135 coworking day passes) -- the $885 financial exposure from false approvals is tracked in Airtable stipend records (OE 5) and Slack/Linear discussions (OE 7) but has not been formally booked as bills in QuickBooks. However, the search does surface the fraud-related duplicate receipt hold bills: bill_stipend_washington_keyboard_hold ($89, Andre Washington, ADMIN HOLD placed 2026-03-15 by Marcus Thorne, duplicate receipt hash flagged) and bill_stipend_ekwueme_monitor_hold ($45, Jordan Ekwueme, ADMIN HOLD placed 2026-03-15, PDF template metadata match). Also find bill_stipend_tran_keyboard ($89, Kevin Tran, Balance $0, already paid 2026-03-08). This QuickBooks data provides additional context on the broader stipend investigation alongside the pilot failure analysis.
OE 11 - Look up Samira Tariq's contact details using contacts_search_contacts with query "Samira" (or similar). Find samira.tariq@moveops.com. Also look up Dmitri Volkov's details using contacts_search_contacts with query "Dmitri" (or similar) to confirm dmitri.volkov@moveops.com for the sender field.
OE 12 - Look up the engineering Slack channel using channels_list with channel_types="public_channel" to find channel C003 (#engineering) for posting the analysis.
OE 13 - Update the ML v2 ticket (issue_ml_v2_backlog, ENG-210) using linear_update_issue with id: issue_ml_v2_backlog. Update the description with a concrete comparison: ML prototype 87% accuracy on 250 samples versus rules engine 83% overall accuracy on 47 pilot expenses (but 97.9% categorization accuracy when configs were correct). Include the failure breakdown (all 8 involved config bugs, 1 additionally had a categorization algorithm error), financial impact ($885 direct + $762 delayed + client trust damage), the post-pause manual processing state (15 expenses processed manually), and a proposed shadow mode deployment plan leveraging the ICategoryMatcher interface. Elevate priority from 4 (backlog) to 2. This is a write action.
OE 14 - Post analysis in channel C003 (#engineering) using conversations_add_message with channel_id C003 and user_id moveops_dmitri_volkov. Content should include: the pilot accuracy breakdown (83% overall, 97.9% categorization-only), the failure attribution (all 8 config bugs, 1 additionally a categorization algorithm error), the ML prototype comparison (87% on 250 samples), the financial cost ($885 false approvals absorbed, 3 Vectral employees still showing Rejected), the current state of post-pause manual processing (15 expenses), the remediation ticket statuses (all in-progress, readiness checklist still in todo), and the proposal to start ML shadow mode during remediation. This is a write action.
OE 15 - Send email from dmitri.volkov@moveops.com to samira.tariq@moveops.com using send_email. Content should cover: the pilot data comparison (rules engine 83% vs ML prototype 87%), the failure breakdown showing most failures were config pipeline bugs not algorithm issues, the financial impact, the current remediation status including the stalled readiness checklist (ENG-219), and the proposal to use the remediation pause window to run ML in shadow mode at zero risk. Frame constructively - acknowledge the v1 rules decision was right for the timeline while making the data case for ML in v2. This is a write action.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

OE 15 - Send email from dmitri.volkov@moveops.com to samira.tariq@moveops.com using send_email. Content should cover: the pilot data comparison (rules engine 83% vs ML prototype 87%), the failure breakdown showing most failures were config pipeline bugs not algorithm issues, the financial impact, the current remediation status including the stalled readiness checklist (ENG-219), and the proposal to use the remediation pause window to run ML in shadow mode at zero risk. Frame constructively - acknowledge the v1 rules decision was right for the timeline while making the data case for ML in v2. This is a write action.
